new_tasks.title: Prove iter_F leaves subformula closure at bounded depth
new_tasks.description:
Prove that for any formula phi, the iterated F-application iter_F n phi eventually leaves the subformula closure. Specifically:

1. Define or compute the maximum F-nesting depth in closureWithNeg(phi)
2. Prove that iter_F (max_depth + 1) phi is NOT in closureWithNeg(phi) for appropriate max_depth
3. This provides the foundation for proving boundedness: in any RestrictedMCS over phi, the sequence iter_F 1 phi, iter_F 2 phi, ... must eventually exit the closure

Key infrastructure to use:
- closureWithNeg : Formula -> Finset Formula (finite!)
- iter_F_complexity : complexity (iter_F n phi) = 5 * n + complexity phi
- Formula.subformulas and closure membership lemmas

Deliverables:
- Lemma iter_F_leaves_closure : forall phi n, n > max_F_depth phi -> iter_F n phi not in closureWithNeg phi
- Supporting lemmas for max_F_depth computation or bound

new_tasks.title: Prove succ_chain_fam MCS have bounded F-depth
new_tasks.description:
Prove that the specific MCS in succ_chain_fam construction have bounded F-iteration depth. This is the targeted fix for f_nesting_is_bounded and p_nesting_is_bounded.

The succ_chain_fam construction builds MCS at integer indices where:
- forward_chain uses successor_exists to place F-witnesses at the NEXT index
- Each step in the chain 'consumes' one level of F-nesting

Proof strategy:
1. Show that the succ_chain_fam construction places F-witnesses at bounded depth by construction
2. Formalize that if F(phi) in M_n, then phi's witness is at a bounded distance in the chain
3. Use the closure depth bound from Task 1 to characterize what 'bounded' means
4. Replace the sorry in f_nesting_is_bounded with the actual proof
5. Apply symmetric argument for p_nesting_is_bounded

Key infrastructure:
- forward_chain, backward_chain definitions
- successor_exists, predecessor_exists theorems
- bounded_witness theorem (already uses iter_F boundary pattern)
- Closure depth lemma from Task 1

Success criteria:
- f_nesting_is_bounded and p_nesting_is_bounded proven without sorry
- Full lake build succeeds

new_tasks.title: FMP-based boundedness proof (fallback)
new_tasks.description:
FALLBACK TASK: Only pursue if Task 2 (succ_chain_fam bounded F-depth) encounters fundamental obstacles.

Connect the succ_chain_fam construction to the existing FMP infrastructure to prove boundedness via the Finite Model Property.

The FMP ensures that if a formula is satisfiable, it's satisfiable in a finite model with bounded size. In finite models, F-chains must terminate.

Proof strategy:
1. Show that succ_chain_fam MCS correspond to points in a filtrated model
2. Connect to ClosureMCS from FMP infrastructure
3. Use the finite model bound to establish F-depth bound
4. Lift the bound back to the canonical model construction

Existing infrastructure in Theories/Bimodal/Metalogic/Decidability/FMP/:
- ClosureMCS, FiniteModel types
- Filtration construction
- TruthPreservation theorems
- fmp_contrapositive theorem

This approach is heavyweight but provides the full model-theoretic justification. Should only be pursued if simpler approaches fail.

Success criteria:
- Proof of f_nesting_is_bounded and p_nesting_is_bounded via FMP connection
- Full lake build succeeds
- No additional axioms or sorries introduced

analysis_summary: The blocker is that f_nesting_is_bounded and p_nesting_is_bounded require model-theoretic reasoning to prove F/P-iterations are bounded in MCS. Proposed 3 tasks: (1) prove iter_F leaves closures at bounded depth, (2) prove succ_chain_fam has bounded F-depth using closure bounds, (3) fallback to FMP connection if simpler approaches fail.
